title: Jefferson Morales (Earth-1610)
content:

71st Precinct |marital Status = Married |partner = [[Rio Morales (Earth-1610)|Rio Morales]] (wife) |relatives = * [[Aaron Davis (Earth-1610)|Aaron Davis]] † (brother) * [[Miles Morales (Earth-1610)|Miles Morales]] (son) * [[Gloria Morales]] (mother-in-law) * [[Maria Morales (Earth-1610)|Maria Morales]] (sister-in-law) |species = Human |ethnicity = African American |citizenship = {{Citizenship|USA}} |gender = Male |skin color = Black |eyes = Brown |hair = Black |height = 183 cm |weight = 70 kg |appearances = * ''[[Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse]]'' * ''[[The Spider Within: A Spider-Verse Story]]'' * ''[[Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse]]'' * ''[[Spider-Man: Beyond the Spider-Verse]]'' (upcoming) |voice actor = [[Brian Tyree Henry]] |actor =}} {{Quote|I know I don't always do what [[Miles Morales|you]] need me to do or say what you need me to say. I see this spark in you. It's amazing. It's why I push you. But it's yours. Whatever you choose to do with it, you'll be great.|[[Jefferson Morales (Earth-1610)|Jefferson Morales]]|Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse}} Officer '''Jefferson "Jeff" Morales''' (formerly '''Davis''') is an police officer in Brooklyn and a member of the Police Department of New York. He is also the father of [[Miles Morales (Earth-1610)|Miles Morales]]. Unbeknownst to him, his son took up the mantle of the late [[Peter Parker (Earth-1610)|Spider-Man]], whose acts of vigilantism Jeff used to not appreciate. ==Biography== ===Early Life=== {{Biography Quote|I got pulled in a lot of different directions when I was young|Jefferson Davis|Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse}} [[File:Aaron and Jeff (E-1610) 001.png|thumb|left]] When he and his brother, [[Aaron Davis (Earth-1610)|Aaron]], were young, they used to tag walls with graffiti according to his own word he and his brother were the kind of people their neighborhood wouldn't want to mess with. When Jeff started on the path to becoming a law enforcer, he and Aaron drifted apart. Jeff went on to fall in love with [[Rio Morales (Earth-1610)|Rio Morales]] and had a son with her, whom they named [[Miles Morales (Earth-1610)|Miles]]. After having Miles, the two decided to get married. Despite being estranged from Aaron, they did not completely cut each other off and Miles often visited him. When [[Peter Parker (Earth-1610)|Spider-Man]] began saving the city, Jeff was among the few police officers who were miffed by the interference and popularity of the vigilante. At the same time he began to get a huge love for his son wanting him to do special things and not make mistakes such as his brother and he did on their youth.''[[Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse]]''''[[Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse]]'' ===[[Collider Crisis]]=== Jeff saw talent in his son and pushed him to better himself at his new school, the [[Brooklyn Visions Academy]], causing some friction between the two of them. Despite this, the two had a loving relationship. One evening, Miles came home distraught, unbeknownst to them that he had witnessed the death of Spider-Man, Jeff and Rio assumed that it was because of school stress, and though they allowed him to stay home instead of return to his dorm room, Jeff expressed some level of disapproval. Miles disappeared shortly after this and wasn't heard from for quite a while, worrying both Jeff and Rio. Jeff became desperate enough that he called his brother in hopes of finding him. [[File:Aaron and Jeff (E-1610) 002.png|thumb|left]] The next day while on patrol, Jeff answered a call to a disturbance involving multiple [[Spider-People]]. Jeff spotted a figure swinging away with a body and followed, only to find a boy in a Spider-Man merch costume in front of a body. The boy disappears and Jeff identified the body as his brother. Furious and in mourning, he put out an APB on "a new Spider-Man." Jeff then went to Miles' dorm and tried to apologize and explain the way he'd been acting; seeing the moving shadow under his door, Jeff just assumed that he was being ignored. [[File:Jeff watching Spidey vs Kingpin (Miles E-1610) 001.png|thumb]] Later that evening, Jeff saw the dimensional anomaly occurring around [[Fisk Tower]]. Jeff helped people evacuate and ended up in a room with a view of the battle between [[Wilson Fisk (Earth-1610)|Wilson Fisk]] and the new Spider-Man, urging the latter to get up when he seemed defeated. Once the city was safe, Jeff went back outside, having placed the Kingpin's lackey [[Lonnie Lincoln (Earth-1610)|Tombstone]] under arrest and soon received a call from Miles. The call got cut and Jeff was then approached by Spider-Man himself, who then hugged him shortly after their greetings, much to his surprise. Though Jeff evidently developed a newfound respect for this version of Spider-Man after witnessing his actions to save the city, Jeff tried to downplay it by saying he didn't approve of his methods but that they would just need to agree to disagree. [[File:Miles' art 001.png|thumb]] Jeff continued to try to repair his relationship with Miles, even taking him to a wall — presumably the privately - owned one he promised Miles over the phone - to tag with his art, this one in honor of Aaron.''[[Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse]]'' ===Meeting With Weber=== ''To be added'' ====Duel in Brooklyn==== ''To be added'' ===Promotional Party=== ''To be added'' ===Talk with Gwen=== ''To be added'' ==Personality== {{Biography Quote|Don't cops run red lights?"
"Oh, yeah, some do. But not your dad.|[[Miles Morales (Earth-1610)|Miles Morales]] and Jefferson Davis|Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse}} Jefferson is a overbearing and loving father. As a result; he wants to; make sure that; Miles has better opportunities than he had. ==Abilities== *'''Bilingualism:''' Jefferson is fluent in his native English, as well as Spanish. It's implied that, he was forced to learned the language, by his wife once they began dating. ==Relationships== ===Family=== *[[Aaron Davis (Earth-1610)|Aaron Davis/Prowler]] † - Brother *[[Rio Morales (Earth-1610)|Rio Morales]] - Wife *[[Miles Morales (Earth-1610)|Miles Morales/Spider-Man]] - Son *[[Gloria Morales]] - Mother-In-Law *[[Maria Morales (Earth-1610)|Maria Morales]] - sister-in-law ===Friends=== * [[Calleros]] ===Allies=== *[[Peter Parker (Earth-1610)|Peter Parker/Spider-Man]] † *[[Gwen Stacy (Earth-65)|Gwanda]] - Son's best friend ===Enemies=== *[[Wilson Fisk (Earth-1610)|Wilson Fisk/Kingpin]] *[[Jonathan Ohnn|Johnathan Ohnn/Spot]] ==Trivia== *Jeff's in-movie contacts list contains several Easter eggs, featuring names of several real-world Spider-Man-related Marvel creators, namely and listed in order of appearance: [[w:c:marvel:Marc Webb|M. Webb]], [[w:c:marvel:Denys Cowan|D. Cowan]], [[w:c:marvel:Chris McKenna|C. McKenna]], [[w:c:marvel:Todd McFarlane|T. McFarlane]], and [[w:c:marvel:Steve Ditko|Steve Ditko]]. *In ''Across the Spider-Verse'' it is revealed that before Miles got bitten by a radio-active spider, he had inherited Jefferson's athletic abilities. With Jeff struggling to climb a water tower near his apartment and his general fear of heights used as a callback joke. *Jeff along with his wife, [[Rio Morales (Earth-1610)|Rio Morales]] believe [[Gwen Stacy (Earth-65)|Gwen Stacy]]'s real name to be "Gwanda". ==References== {{DEFAULTSORT:Morales, Jefferson}} [[Category:Characters]] [[Category:Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse characters]] [[Category:Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse characters]] [[Category:Spider-Man: Beyond the Spider-Verse characters]] [[Category:Male characters]] [[Category:Jefferson Davis]] [[Category:African Americans]] [[Category:Americans]] [[Category:Police Officers]] [[Category:Married]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Earth-1610]]
